sources.name: SEC EDGAR: Coherent Fiscal 2026 Q3 Form 10-Q

metrics.label: Revenue
metrics.sourceText: Revenues $1,805,641 for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared with $1,497,879 for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

metrics.label: Cost of revenue
metrics.sourceText: Cost of goods sold was $1,125,696 for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared with $970,189 for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

metrics.label: Net earnings attributable to Coherent Corp.
metrics.sourceText: Net Earnings Attributable to Coherent Corp. was $191,402 for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared with $15,711 for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

metrics.label: Datacenter & Communications revenue
metrics.sourceText: Datacenter & Communications revenues increased 41% to $1,362 million, compared to $969 million for the same period in the prior fiscal year.
metrics.sourceSection: Management Discussion and Analysis / Results of Operations / Segment Results

metrics.label: Datacenter & Communications segment profit
metrics.sourceText: Datacenter & Communications segment profit increased 49% to $348 million, compared to $233 million for the same period last fiscal year.
metrics.sourceSection: Management Discussion and Analysis / Results of Operations / Segment Results

metrics.label: Industrial revenue
metrics.sourceText: Industrial revenues decreased 16% to $444 million, compared to $529 million for the same period in the prior fiscal year.
metrics.sourceSection: Management Discussion and Analysis / Results of Operations / Segment Results

supportingContext.value: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and management attributed growth to strong datacenter and communications demand.
Use D&C segment growth as a broad proxy. No individual optical module revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

supportingContext.value: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and management attributed growth to strong datacenter and communications demand.
Use D&C segment growth as a broad proxy. No individual optical module revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.
Management said AI datacenter infrastructure continues to scale and that Coherent is expanding capacity to meet demand.
AI infrastructure is the clearest demand driver, but the sources do not disclose revenue by AI server attachment.

shareOfScope.label: A 在所用收入分类中占比中等（30%-80%）或占比不详

managementAttribution.label: 管理层笼统提及 A 有贡献

shareOfScope.label: A 在所用收入分类中占比中等（30%-80%）或占比不详

managementAttribution.label: 管理层笼统提及 A 有贡献

supportingContext.value: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and management attributed growth to strong datacenter and communications demand.
Use D&C segment growth as a broad proxy. No individual optical module revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.
The presentation names optical networking products, 400G/lane links, optical circuit switch, transceivers, and multi-rail for AI datacenters.
Mapped to the data center networking side of the topology. No switch-specific revenue or growth is disclosed.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: Management said the quarter was driven by exceptionally strong demand across datacenter and communications, and that AI datacenter infrastructure scaling is driving capacity expansion.
qualitativeSignals.notes: This is a qualitative demand signal only; it should not change YoY line width because no AI-server-specific revenue split is disclosed.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: Coherent says it is ramping Datacenter and Communications capacity end to end, is on track to double internal InP output by year-end and more than double again by 2027, and produces EMLs, CW lasers, and photodiodes on a 6-inch platform.
qualitativeSignals.notes: Targets internal capacity and component capability, not standalone external revenue.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The 10-Q says Coherent entered into a multi-year capacity agreement with NVIDIA that may require incremental investments in equipment, labor, and working capital to support future production volumes through 2030.
qualitativeSignals.notes: The source does not name a specific product in the agreement, so the optical module and AI server targets are contextual to the report's datacenter demand narrative.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The presentation lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
qualitativeSignals.notes: Roadmap signal only. It is not treated as current Q3 FY2026 revenue.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The presentation lists transceivers at 1.6T, 3.2T, and 6.4T, DCI transceivers, transport/transmission, optical circuit switch, optical components, and multi-rail as growth engines.
qualitativeSignals.notes: These are named growth engines without individual revenue or growth disclosure.

timelineEvents.title: Coherent Corp.发布 Fiscal 2026 Third Quarter Form 10-Q

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: Coherent is ramping internal InP output and 6-inch wafer demand for AI infrastructure.
companyProductCoverage.notes: The sources describe internal InP output and wafer demand; no external wafer revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces CW lasers alongside EMLs and photodiodes.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as product/capability coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose standalone CW laser revenue, share, or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces EMLs alongside CW lasers and photodiodes.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as product/capability coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose standalone EML revenue, share, or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: Multiple CPO architectures, Silicon Photonics, InP, and VCSEL enable flexible networks.
companyProductCoverage.notes: VCSEL is named as a technology route, but the Q3 sources do not disclose VCSEL revenue, share, or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces photodiodes alongside EMLs and CW lasers.
companyProductCoverage.notes: This generic PD node is used because the source says photodiodes but does not specify silicon integrated PD or EML/DFB PD module revenue.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces photodiodes, and Silicon Photonics is listed among flexible network technologies.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Photodiode capability is named, but no standalone photodiode revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The presentation lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as roadmap coverage. Q3 FY2026 does not disclose current NPO revenue or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: Multiple CPO architectures, Silicon Photonics, InP, and VCSEL enable flexible networks.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Silicon Photonics is named as a technology route; the sources do not isolate modulator revenue or current commercialization.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: Coherent describes end-to-end optical integration for next-generation AI architectures and lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as roadmap/capability coverage. Q3 FY2026 does not disclose silicon photonic engine revenue or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The presentation names multiple CPO architectures and lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Not treated as current Q3 FY2026 external sales because the source places CPO/NPO new revenue in H2 2026.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The Datacenter & Communications segment includes transceivers and modules; the presentation lists transceivers as growth engines.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Broad optical module parent node used because the sources do not split revenue by EML/DFB versus silicon photonic module route.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The Datacenter & Communications segment includes transceivers and modules; transceivers at 1.6T, 3.2T, and 6.4T are listed as growth engines.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Required optical_module child. Kept as route coverage only because the sources do not identify the transceiver route as EML/DFB or disclose EML/DFB module financials.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and the presentation names Silicon Photonics among technologies for flexible networks.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Required optical_module child. Kept as route coverage only because Silicon Photonics is named as a technology route, but no silicon photonic pluggable module financial split is disclosed.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The investor presentation lists DCI Transceivers ZR/ZR+ as growth engines.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as product-line coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose DCI/ZR revenue, share, or growth.

companyProductCoverage.evidenceText: The investor presentation lists Optical Circuit Switch as a growth engine.
companyProductCoverage.notes: Recorded as product-line coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose OCS revenue, share, or growth.
